a.label: carrier O
a.standard_term: carrier/pullback map through which the metric construction must factor (directional normal-curvature object), as opposed to the raw Hessian

b.label: coupling anisotropy A_c
b.standard_term: squared norm of the standard-irrep projection of a 3-vector; equals 9x the sample variance (sum-of-squared-differences identity)

evidence.label: Part VIII — Parity law
evidence.summary:
```
σ_even ∈ ℚ ,   σ_odd ∈ ℚ(√q)      (= Lovelock parity)
```
The even scalar curvature is rational; the mean-curvature / odd orders carry one factor `√q` (the unit normal). It is the `q^((r+2)/2)` normalization, not a d
